one_liner: Ecwid provides a broad REST API (v3) secured via OAuth2 or dashboard-issued secret tokens, but full API access typically requires a paid plan or a manually-requested developer upgrade.

auth_methods.notes: Standard OAuth 2.0 flow for external/public apps; exchange code for access token via POST to https://my.ecwid.com/api/oauth/token. Bearer token required in Authorization header; query param tokens disabled as of March 2025.
For custom, single-store apps, a pre-generated secret access token can be copied directly from the app dashboard, skipping the OAuth flow.

self_serve_notes: Standard free-tier Ecwid stores cannot access the REST API; a paid plan is normally required. However, developers building integrations/public apps can request a free upgrade to a paid test store by emailing Ecwid API support, after which tokens can be self-generated via the app dashboard.

api_surface.notes: Ecwid REST API v3 covers products, orders, customers, inventory, store profile, etc.

main_blocker: API access requires a paid Ecwid plan (or a manually-requested free developer upgrade via emailing support), which limits pure self-serve onboarding for agent toolkits.

evidence.supports: Describes OAuth2 flow and direct secret token access via app dashboard for custom apps.
Confirms Bearer token in Authorization header is now the only supported auth method.
Indicates REST API access requires a paid plan on standard tiers.
REST API overview showing broad API surface across store resources.

flags: Third-party MCP integrations (viaSocket, Vinkius) exist but no official first-party Ecwid MCP server confirmed.
Free API access for developers requires manual email request to support, not fully self-serve.
No sandbox/test mode exists; must use a real (paid or upgraded) store for testing.
